response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with solid software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing), but is a poor fit for this specific role. C++ experience is minimal and dated — roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a university thesis project — far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no SQL Server/Oracle experience (only MongoDB/MySQL), no Windows-platform focus, and no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain exposure. RIDE Capital touches investing but via a Node/TypeScript/GraphQL stack, not capital-markets C++ systems.
strengths: Broad, deep engineering background with strong testing and CI/CD discipline
Some fintech/investment exposure at RIDE Capital and strong communication (talks, workshops)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months) and over a decade old; nowhere near the required 5–10 years
No SQL Server/Oracle and no Windows-platform development focus
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain knowledge
